question: What needs attention now? Turn this Contract Review Tracker into a deadline-risk dashboard that flags what is overdue and due soon: https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/teams/CELASPEAR245/Lists/Contract%20Review%20Tracker%20fake/AllItems.aspx

Save it to contract_deadline_dashboard.html. Don't explore other tools or packages. Render a clickable link in my terminal.

question: Beyond chatting; you can make a game plan you can read, correct, and approve - then let the agent build to your spec.
answer: First outline a brief plan for my review. This is a PLANNING task only - do NOT write or run any application code. Read the local file data/contracts.csv (a snapshot of the Contract Review Tracker with columns Title, Counterparty, AssignedAttorney, ContractValue, DueDate, ContractType, ReviewStatus, Priority). Then produce a concise written engineering plan (about one page, under ~400 words) for an upcoming build: a single self-contained, dependency-free HTML deadline-risk dashboard that shows three summary tiles (count overdue, count due within 30 days, count due later) and a table of all contracts sorted by DueDate with a computed days-until-due, highlighting overdue rows red and rows due within 30 days amber. The plan must cover the steps, the inputs and outputs, the exact data fields used, the edge cases (missing or malformed DueDate values, how today's date is determined), and how you will verify the result is correct - but do NOT build the dashboard. Save the plan to a file named plan.md, keeping the whole plan concise enough to read at a glance. In your final message, render a clickable link to the saved plan.md file and paste its full contents so I can review and approve before any code is written. Don't explore other tools or packages.

question: Plan it, then build it for real - an interactive deadline-risk view of the contract tracker. A spec you executed, not a vibe.
answer: First outline a brief plan for my review, then carry it out. Read the local file data/contracts.csv (columns Title, Counterparty, AssignedAttorney, ContractValue, DueDate, ContractType, ReviewStatus, Priority). Write and run a Python script build_dashboard.py that generates a single self-contained, dependency-free HTML deadline-risk dashboard and saves it to contract_deadline_dashboard.html: three summary tiles (count overdue, count due within 30 days, count due later) and a table of all contracts sorted by DueDate showing Title, Counterparty, AssignedAttorney, DueDate, Priority and a computed days-until-due based on today's date, with overdue rows highlighted red and rows due within 30 days amber. Don't explore other tools or packages. In your final message, render a clickable link to the saved contract_deadline_dashboard.html file and confirm the three tile counts.

question: Make it reproducible and trustworthy: plan it, build it, prove the numbers with tests, and hand a colleague a one-command rebuild.
answer: First outline a brief plan for my review, then carry it out. Working only from the local file data/contracts.csv, produce a reproducible deadline-risk dashboard inside a dashboard/ folder: (1) build_dashboard.py that reads data/contracts.csv and writes a single self-contained, dependency-free dashboard/index.html with three summary tiles (count overdue, count due within 30 days, count due later) and a table of all contracts sorted by DueDate with a computed days-until-due, overdue rows red and rows due within 30 days amber; (2) test_dashboard.py with pytest tests that assert the overdue count and the days-until-due calculation are correct for known rows; (3) a README.md a non-technical colleague can follow to regenerate the dashboard. Put all of build_dashboard.py, test_dashboard.py, README.md and index.html inside the dashboard/ folder. Run the build, then run the tests. Don't explore other tools or packages. In your final message, list the files you created, paste the complete verbatim passing pytest output, and render a clickable link to dashboard/index.html.

question: Vibe-code it: a live world map of Microsoft, Amazon & Google data centers
answer: Create a modern, professional single-page website with an interactive world map of known Microsoft, Amazon, and Google data centers. Show counts by country and state, support zoom and pan, and let me filter by company and country. Use trusted, cited sources. Where credible power-consumption figures exist, add a dynamic report that updates as I change the filters.

question: Plan-mode build: an interactive data center map with filters + power reports
answer: Work in plan mode: propose a short plan and wait for my approval before writing any code. Then build a modern single-page site with an interactive world map of Microsoft, Amazon (AWS), and Google data centers - counts by country and region, zoom and pan, click a marker for detail, and live filters by company and country. Add power-consumption reports that recompute from the active filters where credible data exists. Use trusted, cited sources, save the data to a local file the site loads from, and include a short README.

question: Production build: a full Global Data Center Intelligence dashboard
answer: Work in plan mode: propose a short plan and wait for my approval before building. Then create a polished 'Global Data Center Explorer' that maps Microsoft (Azure), Amazon (AWS), and Google (GCP) facilities - clustered markers, zoom and pan, detail on click, and live filters by company, country, and free-text search. Add power reports that recompute from the filters, plus one extra cited dataset (e.g. grid carbon intensity) in the detail panel. Add an 'Ask the data' chat box, served behind a tiny local server, that sends my question plus a compact summary of the currently filtered data to the local Copilot CLI in one shot (agency copilot -p "...") and renders its one-shot reply; if the CLI isn't available, degrade gracefully with a clear message. Store the data in a local file with a refresh script and a small validation test you run and show passing. Include a README covering how to start the server and use the chat.
